note: Minimal first-pass format -- one event per notable thing that happened, in order. Not a designed schema; the real event_log format is a separate future conversation, this just captures this run's content.

events.summary: check_input.py failed: all 5 required input files missing from input/.
User's existing input files had non-standard names (healthcare_sttm.xlsx, hierarchy.md, udf_definitions.md); renamed to required filenames.
sttm.xlsx header 'Transformation Logic / UDF Call' didn't match any recognized transformation_note alias.
Added 'transformation logic / udf call' as a recognized alias in parse_sttm.py rather than asking user to rename their spreadsheet column.
All input checks pass; connectivity to Postgres confirmed; generation/review/execution/validation all WARN (not yet run).
Schema Parser: source_db (schema raw) and target_db (schema analytics) both explicit in input docs, no fallback needed.
STTM Parser: 70 mapping rows classified (DIRECT/UDF/EXPRESSION), zero duplicate_mapping or NEEDS_REVIEW.
Dependency Builder: 5 tables, no cross-table LOOKUP edges (all FK columns pass through raw business keys), no cycles.
target_schema.md declares dim_patients/dim_doctors as SCD2 and fct_appointments as incremental, but render_sqlx.py only supports load_strategy=full_load.
User chose full_load-for-now with loud flagging in buildspec.grain and generated SQLX headers; real SCD2/incremental support deferred.
4 columns (patient_dk/hashdiff, doctor_dk/hashdiff) use nested UDF calls the buildspec's flat UDF transformation type can't express.
Reclassified as EXPRESSION with fully-qualified raw SQL instead of forcing NEEDS_REVIEW.
SQLX Generator and Artifact Generator completed; 15 .sqlx files, bootstrap/, all metadata written; 5 target tables.
30/32 checks PASS; 2 WARN (schema_cross_check, no Postgres MCP tool available in session).
User expected a Postgres MCP tool; none was loaded (checked ToolSearch repeatedly, .mcp.json unreadable).
User restarted the MCP connection; mcp__postgres__query became available, confirmed connected to 'postgres' db not the project's 3 databases.
python -m engine.dbadmin seed reset source_db/intermediate_db/target_db (confirmed empty beforehand via MCP query, safe to proceed).
Attempt 1: bootstrap failed -- gen_bootstrap.py emitted CREATE TABLE raw.doctors (FK to facilities) before raw.facilities existed; DDL order wasn't FK-topologically sorted.
Added topological_create_order() to gen_bootstrap.py, used only for the two ddl.sql.tmpl renders; regenerated bootstrap.
Attempt 2: bootstrap failed on target DDL -- fct_appointments/dim_insurance FK'd to dim_patients.patient_bk / dim_doctors.doctor_bk, neither of which is unique (SCD2 business keys).
Dropped the DB-level FK constraint for those 2 business-key references in target_schema.json (kept as documented lineage only); facility_id FK (genuinely unique) left intact.
Attempt 3: pipeline failed at dim_doctors.read -- udf_generate_dk(varchar, timestamptz) does not exist; CURRENT_TIMESTAMP is timestamptz but UDF param is plain TIMESTAMP.
Added explicit CURRENT_TIMESTAMP::TIMESTAMP cast in the 2 affected buildspec EXPRESSION strings; re-rendered SQLX.
Attempt 4: pipeline failed at dim_doctors.read -- value too long for doctor_bk VARCHAR(20); udf.md's doctor_bk formula (doctor_id + '|' + license_number) needs ~50 chars.
User chose to widen doctor_bk to VARCHAR(50) in target_schema.md and live DB (ALTER COLUMN, since CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS wouldn't retroactively widen it).
Full pipeline succeeded: 15/15 steps, 46 rows total across 5 tables, 1.3s total runtime.
Attempt 1: crashed immediately -- validate_data() used bare unqualified table names, only works for public-schema projects (this one uses raw/staging/analytics).
Added --intermediate-schema CLI param to engine/validate.py, schema-qualified every live query (staging, target, source, FK-referenced tables).
Attempt 2: G-manifest-drift WARN listed 35 files as 'missing' despite existing -- manifest.json used output/-prefixed paths, doubled against project_root.
Stripped redundant output/ prefix from manifest.json's generated_files paths, matching the skill's reference-fixture convention.
Attempt 2 (same run): D-udf-dim_patients.* FAIL (10/12 or 4/12 mismatches per column) -- join-key heuristic picked 'gender' (low-cardinality DIRECT column), silently colliding rows.
Rewrote join-key selection to use the source table's declared PRIMARY KEY (direct passthrough preferred, single-arg UDF-of-PK fallback); WARN honestly when no safe key exists instead of guessing.
Final run: 222 PASS, 1 honest WARN (D-udf-dim_doctors -- doctor_bk needs 2 source columns, no safe single-key correlation), 0 FAIL.
Wrote docs/session/2026-07-18-healthcare-etl.md summarizing all fixes and gaps for the repo owner; restructured docs/ into session/ (narrative) and event_log/ (this file).
